characters.name: Three haloed saints (possibly including Saint John the Baptist)

event: Three Saints — Fresco at Avigliana, San Giovanni
symbols: golden halos — the sanctity of the depicted saints
red and gold robes — the traditional colors of martyrdom and divine glory
three figures together — the communion of saints
description: A weathered medieval fresco showing three standing haloed figures against a green-grey background. The leftmost figure wears a red robe and red head covering. The central figure is partially obscured by damage but wears dark garments. The rightmost figure wears a golden-yellow robe with a white veil or head covering. All three have golden halos. The fresco surface is significantly damaged and worn, with areas of lost paint revealing the wall beneath. The style is simple and frontal, typical of medieval Italian church frescoes.
